uploadResume.raw_text:
.
Jeriah Samuel
Wednesbury, West Midlands WS10 7DQ    |   07758659895    |   jeriah_samuel@yahoo.com
Summary
A responsible, driven and charismatic individual with extensive experience in 3D tutoring and troubleshooting, handling broadband faults and billing issues in a call centre environment. I take a professional approach to work, working ardently to meet and exceed set objectives whilst providing the highest quality, personal service possible to clients.
Experience
Freelance 3D Artist 04/2016 - Current 
Upwork 
Taught clients how to do a variety of tasks in Blender 3D. Introduced tools and workflows in an ad-hoc manner to ease learning. Took users from beginner to a level of comfort where they had the ability to tackle their own projects from start to finish. 
Crafted educational materials to teach users how to navigate 3D software via both pre recorded and live formats.
Troubleshooted a variety of client issues when using Blender.

BT Customer Care Advisor – Faults and Billing 01/2018 - 09/2018
Handling customer queries focused mainly on the start of their customer journey.
Diagnosing and rectifying a variety of broadband, landline and tv faults using in house systems.
Handling customer complaints and going above and beyond to resolve appropriately for both the customer and the business.
Integrating business goals into workflow so that customer queries can be resolved on budget whilst also providing a personal ,simple and brilliant experience for the customer

BT Work Placement - contact centre  11/2017 - 12/2017
Understanding and integrating the 5 steps for brilliant customer service.
Active listening when dealing with customers along with problem solving both current and future problems.
Taking ownership of problems and commitments with both colleagues and customers.
Core
Skills
Education
Birmingham City University, Birmingham, 2013
HNC Computer Science
GCSEs, 2008
Shireland Collegiate Academy, Smethwick, West Midlands 
8 GCSEs, grade A-C, including Maths (B) and English (A).ICT BTEC Level 2 grade merit.
Accomplishments
Interests
Creative Pursuits - I like to dabble with creative writing, I’m currently writing a novel (15, 000 words and growing) and also playing with creative concepts in blender. I also do a little drumming.
Sports - I like to keep fit and play football regularly, entering leagues and tournaments with friends and colleagues.
The Usual Suspects - Social Functions, festivals, concerts, travelling.

why_suggested.3D Tutor: The current title "Freelance 3D Artist" directly supports a move into "3D Tutor" because the work experience explicitly states that the candidate taught clients how to do a variety of tasks in Blender 3D. Tool evidence is strong because "Blender 3D" and "3D software" are named directly, and the resume also states that the candidate introduced tools and workflows and troubleshooted client issues when using Blender. Skill evidence is also clear from phrases such as "3D tutoring," "Crafted educational materials," and teaching users through both pre recorded and live formats. The inferred Mid-Level seniority is supported by the ongoing freelance experience from 04/2016 - Current, and this role is a direct progression because it formalises work the candidate is already doing in a client-facing teaching capacity.
why_suggested.Customer Service Advisor: The title "BT Customer Care Advisor – Faults and Billing" aligns directly with the future role "Customer Service Advisor" because it is essentially the same customer-facing service track in standard market terms. The resume provides skill evidence through "Handling customer queries," "Handling customer complaints," "Active listening," and "Understanding and integrating the 5 steps for brilliant customer service," all of which map closely to customer service work. There is also domain evidence from resolving issues at the start of the customer journey and integrating business goals into workflow while providing a personal service experience. Mid-Level seniority is defensible because the candidate has both direct BT customer care experience and earlier contact centre placement exposure, making this a realistic continuation within the same industry context.
why_suggested.Technical Support Advisor: The role "Technical Support Advisor" is supported by the candidate's existing titles and responsibilities across both freelance and BT experience. In the BT role, the resume explicitly states "Diagnosing and rectifying a variety of broadband, landline and tv faults using in house systems," and in freelance work it states "Troubleshooted a variety of client issues when using Blender," which provides multi-context troubleshooting evidence. Relevant skills include "troubleshooting," "problem solving," and "handling broadband faults," while tool and technology evidence includes "Blender 3D" and "3D software" as examples of software support exposure. The inferred Mid-Level seniority fits because the candidate already has hands-on fault diagnosis and user issue resolution experience, making technical support a realistic adjacent progression from both customer care and software tutoring work.

whySuggested.3D Tutor: The role is directly supported by the current title "Freelance 3D Artist" and by the resume statement that the candidate "Taught clients how to do a variety of tasks in Blender 3D." Tool evidence is clear because "Blender 3D" and "Blender" are explicitly named, and the work involved teaching users how to navigate 3D software and troubleshooting client issues. Skill evidence includes "3D tutoring" and "troubleshooting," which align closely with a tutoring role focused on practical software instruction. The inferred Mid-Level seniority is supported by the ongoing freelance work since 04/2016, and this makes 3D Tutor a realistic continuation of the candidate's existing work rather than a speculative jump. In the United Kingdom market, "3D Tutor" is a standard title and represents a direct progression from freelance teaching activity already described in the resume.
whySuggested.Customer Service Advisor: This role is directly aligned with the title "BT Customer Care Advisor – Faults and Billing" and is further supported by the earlier "BT Work Placement - contact centre" experience. The resume provides strong skill evidence through explicit references to "customer service," "Active listening," "problem solving," and handling customer complaints and queries. Although no named customer service platforms are listed, the candidate's experience diagnosing issues and working within a call centre environment supports a customer-facing advisory role. The inferred Mid-Level seniority is conservative and appropriate because the candidate has direct customer care experience plus related contact centre exposure, but there is no explicit management signal. In the United Kingdom, "Customer Service Advisor" is a standard market title and is a realistic next-step or lateral role from the BT customer care background.
whySuggested.Technical Support Advisor: The role is supported by the title "BT Customer Care Advisor – Faults and Billing" together with resume evidence about "Diagnosing and rectifying a variety of broadband, landline and tv faults using in house systems." Additional title alignment comes from the freelance work where the candidate "Troubleshooted a variety of client issues when using Blender," showing technical issue resolution in a second context. Relevant skill evidence includes "troubleshooting," "handling broadband faults," and "problem solving," all of which map well to technical support responsibilities. The inferred Mid-Level seniority fits because the candidate has hands-on fault diagnosis experience, but there is no evidence for a senior or lead support role. In the United Kingdom market, "Technical Support Advisor" is a realistic adjacent role because it builds directly on both telecom fault handling and software-related troubleshooting experience.
whySuggested.3D Artist: This role is directly supported by the existing title "Freelance 3D Artist," making it one of the most evidence-grounded future roles available from the resume. Tool evidence is explicit through "Blender 3D" and "Blender," and the work history shows practical use of 3D software while helping clients complete tasks and projects. Skill evidence includes "3D tutoring" and "troubleshooting," which do not replace artistic production evidence but do show sustained hands-on familiarity with the software environment used by 3D artists. The inferred Mid-Level seniority is appropriate because the freelance role has been current since 04/2016, indicating ongoing involvement without explicit signals for senior specialisation or leadership. In industry terms, this is a direct continuation within the candidate's existing 3D domain and is therefore a realistic role to pursue in the United Kingdom.
